extraction.one_line_summary: YATES Simon won the Budapest ITT by 3 seconds over VANDERPOELE Mathieu, beating former world champion DUMOULIN by 5 seconds on a technical 25-turn course.
tactical_patterns: Punchier riders outperformed pure TT specialists on technical 25-turn urban course with 1.3km uphill finish.
YATES gained entire advantage on final 1.3km climb after being slightly slower at climb base, exploiting gradient.
KEMNA Leonard jumped from youth-level promise to pro breakthrough on technical course, suggesting equipment/position changes pay off urban ITTs.

rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: First career GT time trial win despite only one prior career ITT victory, suggesting technical courses unlock his climbing power
rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: positive: 5 seconds off winner after comeback suggests form returning, though beaten by climber on gradient finish
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: positive: Third place finish massively exceeded pre-race expectations of time loss, indicating late-career ITT resilience
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: neutral: 30 seconds down considered 'solid' performance reveals GC expectations already managing ITT weakness
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: positive: 16 seconds to Yates shows marked improvement over typical ITT performances for climbing-focused rider
rider_signals.SOBRERO Matteo: neutral: Italian champion beaten by non-specialists confirms technical parcours favor power-to-weight over pure speed
